title: michelia pilifera
intro: Michelia pilifera, also known as the Khasi holly or Khasi cedar, is a plant species belonging to the Magnoliaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in India and Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Michelia pilifera include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and wound healer. Herbal preparations of Michelia pilifera can be made into teas, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
